prompt:
Ticket from the workflow-platform board. Our authoring CLI has no linter for workflow documents, so malformed ones sail into the runner and die at execution time with useless errors. I want flowlint.py with a single entry point:

    lint(text: str) -> list[str]

It loads the YAML itself (PyYAML is pinned in requirements.txt — create the project venv with python3 -m venv .venv and .venv/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt; CI runs bash run_verify.sh, which refuses to run without .venv). Assume the input is always well-formed YAML — the editor guarantees that much — the linter owns STRUCTURE.

Every finding is one string "<path>: <message>". Return the findings sorted with plain sorted(); return [] for a clean document. One exception: if the document root is not a mapping, return exactly ["workflow must be a mapping"] and nothing else. Paths are dotted with [i] for list positions: document.dsl, do[2].route.switch[1].then, do[0].sweep.for.do[1].hold.wait.

Top level — allowed sections: document, do, input, output, timeout. Anything else: "K: unknown top-level section". document and do are required: "document: required section is missing" / "do: required section is missing".

document — must be a mapping ("document: must be a mapping"; skip its fields if not). dsl is required ("document.dsl: required field is missing") and must be exactly the STRING "1.0": anything else — including the classic mistake where an unquoted 1.0 loads from YAML as a float — is "document.dsl: must be the string \"1.0\"". namespace and name are required non-empty strings: missing gives "document.X: required field is missing", present-but-wrong gives "document.X: must be a non-empty string". version and description are optional but must be non-empty strings when present (same must-be message). Any other key: "document.K: unknown field".

input / output (top level and on tasks) — must be a mapping with exactly the one key from, else "PATH: must be a mapping with only a \"from\" key". timeout (top level and on tasks) — must be a mapping with exactly the key after, else "PATH: must be a mapping with only an \"after\" key"; after must be a non-empty string, else "PATH.after: must be a non-empty string".

do blocks (top level and inside for) — must be a list ("PATH: must be a list") and non-empty ("PATH: must not be empty"). Each entry is a mapping with exactly one key, the task name: wrong shape is "do[i]: task entry must be a mapping with exactly one key" (skip the entry). The name must be a non-empty string: "do[i]: task name must be a non-empty string" (skip). From here paths run do[i].NAME. A name already used earlier in the SAME block flags the second and later occurrences with "do[i].NAME: duplicate task name" — but still validate the body. The body must be a mapping: "do[i].NAME: task must be a mapping" (skip).

Task types — exactly one of: call, run, set, switch, for, raise, emit, wait. None present: "do[i].NAME: task must declare exactly one task type (found none)". Several: "do[i].NAME: task must declare exactly one task type (found call, set)" — names alphabetical, joined with comma-space. Common fields allowed on every task: if, input, output, timeout, then, metadata, with. Any other key: "do[i].NAME.k: unknown task field". Common-field checks always run even when the type count is wrong; type-specific checks only run when exactly one type is present.

Common fields — with: on a call task it must be a mapping ("....with: must be a mapping"); on any other single-typed task it is "....with: only valid on call tasks"; when the type count is wrong leave with alone. if: non-empty string ("....if: must be a non-empty string"). then: must be a string ("....then: must be a string") and must be continue, end, or the name of a task in the SAME do block, else "....then: unknown jump target \"X\"" — jump targets never cross block boundaries (a task inside for.do can only target its siblings; body tasks cannot target outer tasks, nor the reverse). metadata: must be a mapping ("....metadata: must be a mapping"). input/output/timeout as above.

Type-specific checks, at path do[i].NAME.<type> — call, wait: non-empty string ("...: must be a non-empty string"). set: non-empty mapping ("...: must be a non-empty mapping"). run: a mapping ("...: must be a mapping") declaring exactly one of shell, script, workflow: "....run: must declare exactly one of shell, script, workflow (found none)" / "(found script, shell)" (alphabetical); other keys are "....run.K: unknown field"; whichever of the three is present must itself be a mapping ("....run.shell: must be a mapping"). raise: a mapping with only an error key ("....raise: must be a mapping with only an \"error\" key"); error must be a mapping ("....raise.error: must be a mapping"); error.status is required ("....error.status: required field is missing") and must be an integer — booleans do NOT count ("....error.status: must be an integer"); error.type and error.title are required non-empty strings (same missing / must-be messages as document fields); error.detail optional non-empty string; other keys "....error.K: unknown field". emit: a mapping ("...: must be a mapping"); type required non-empty string; source optional non-empty string; data is anything; other keys "....emit.K: unknown field". switch: a non-empty list ("...: must be a non-empty list"); each case a mapping ("....switch[j]: case must be a mapping") with only when and then ("....switch[j].K: unknown case field"); then is required ("....switch[j].then: required field is missing"), a string, and a valid jump target — same rules and messages as task then; when is optional but must be a non-empty string, else "....switch[j].when: must be a non-empty expression"; a case WITHOUT when is the default, and the second and later defaults get "....switch[j]: duplicate default case". for: a mapping ("...: must be a mapping"); each and in required non-empty strings; at optional non-empty string; do required ("....for.do: required field is missing") and validated recursively as a full do block with path prefix ....for.do; other keys "....for.K: unknown field".

test_flowlint.py is the acceptance contract: bash run_verify.sh must pass, and the tests, run_verify.sh, and requirements.txt stay as they are. Single module, no CLI needed.
